summary: An outstanding sound‑board matrix capture of a 1970 show highlighted by an epic Dark Star and crystal‑clear performance throughout.
review: The recording is a superb SBD/matrix mix that delivers crisp, well‑balanced audio with only occasional scratchy noise around the 16‑minute jam. Musically the night shines thanks to a transcendent Dark Star, tight St. Stephen, an electrifying Not Fade Away and a raucous Turn On Your Lovelight, all backed by inspired playing from Jerry, Bob and the rhythm section.

ai_rating.rationale: Over thirty detailed reviews uniformly praise both the musical performance (especially Dark Star, St. Stephen, NFA and Lovelight) and the audio quality, with only minor technical quirks noted.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, well‑balanced SBD/matrix mix; occasional faint scratch/metallic noise around the 16‑minute Dark Star jam and a few minor hiccups, but overall superb clarity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows beautifully, with a soaring Dark Star that segues into St. Stephen and NFA, followed by a dramatic Darkness jam before launching into an unforgettable Lovelight; transitions are tight and the energy stays high throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is repeatedly lauded – from a perfect feedback squeal to “beasting” on China Cat and delivering a transcendent Dark Star.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s lead guitar interplay (especially on China Cat) receives high praise; his rhythm work supports the jams superbly.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drums are described as tight, perfectly matched with Garcia’s guitar and driving the high‑octane NFA.
